title: Quercus × kiusiana

preparations: Quercus × kiusiana is not a well-studied plant, but it is a hybrid of Quercus acutissima and Quercus glauca. intro: Quercus × kiusiana, also known as Kius Oak or Kyushu Oak, is a hybrid oak tree species that is native to Japan. It has been traditionally used in Japanese medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Quercus × kiusiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Quercus × kiusiana is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or extract, and is often used to make herbal remedies for colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
